changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_periodic_review to k8s_rbac_roles_periodic_review for clarity
review_notes: The control CM-7 (1) is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves ensuring least functionality and periodic review, which aligns with Kubernetes security practices. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but 'k8s_rbac_periodic_review' was too vague and has been renamed to 'k8s_rbac_roles_periodic_review' to specify that it pertains to RBAC roles. The other functions are well-named and technically accurate, mapping to real Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names specify the resource being reviewed, such as 'roles' in RBAC context.
title: Least Functionality | Periodic Review
step1_reasoning: The control CM-7 (1) focuses on ensuring least functionality and periodic review of configurations. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that network policies are set to deny ingress by default and are periodically reviewed to ensure they are still appropriate. Similarly, RBAC roles and bindings should enforce least privilege and be periodically reviewed to ensure they are not overly permissive. These checks ensure that unnecessary access is not granted, aligning with the control's intent.
